content:
Every Generic Component Starts With Good Intentions

4 min read
6 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

A team creates:
CustomerTableComponentThe component works.
The feature ships.
Everyone is happy.
Then another feature arrives.
It also needs a table.
Someone says:
Let’s make it reusable.
The idea sounds reasonable.
After all:
Why build two tables when one table could serve both features?
The component is generalized.
A few configuration options are added.
Nothing seems wrong.
Yet.
The First Layer Of Flexibility
The original component was simple.
Now it supports:
showFilters
showPagination
showSorting
showExportStill manageable.
The abstraction feels useful.
Developers feel productive.
